text.title: Meta Ads Reporting and Diagnosis Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-meta-reporting methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Reports MUST include CPM, CTR (all + outbound), CPC, CVR, CPA, ROAS, Frequency, Spend.
rule.rationale: Missing any of these blocks diagnosis — every symptom map relies on at least 3 columns.

rule.statement: Run breakdowns by age, placement, device, country every week; flag any segment >25% off campaign average.
rule.rationale: Aggregate Meta CPA hides 2-3x deltas between segments.

rule.statement: Frequency above 2.5 is a creative fatigue signal — rotate creative or expand audience within 48 hours.
rule.rationale: CTR drops ~30% and CPM rises ~25% above frequency 2.5; waiting accelerates fatigue.

rule.statement: Each surfaced issue MUST be mapped to a root cause from the standard map (creative fatigue / audience fatigue / LP drop-off / attribution shift / seasonality) before an action is proposed.
rule.rationale: Without root-cause assignment, actions are guesses; track effect per cause class.

rule.statement: Every action item MUST carry expected impact estimate (CPA delta or ROAS delta) and an owner.
rule.rationale: Unowned, unquantified recs never get executed.
